articles.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and enhancing domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key components include incentives for green hydrogen production, establishment of green hydrogen corridors, and support for R&D in electrolysis technology.
• This phase emphasizes skill development and job creation in the emerging green hydrogen ecosystem across the country.
• The move aligns with India's ambitious climate targets and its vision for 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in the energy sector.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Full Disclosure of Electoral Bond Data to Enhance Transparency
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, mandated the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure complete disclosure of all electoral bond transactions, including donor identities and recipient political parties.
• The judgment emphasized the citizens' right to information and the importance of transparency in electoral funding to uphold free and fair elections.
• This decision comes after a series of petitions challenging the anonymity provisions of the Electoral Bond Scheme, citing concerns over potential quid pro quo arrangements.
• The Court directed the ECI to publish all past and future data on its official website within a stipulated timeframe.
• The ruling is expected to significantly impact political funding practices and accountability mechanisms in India.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level, citing a cautious approach to economic recovery.
• The decision was influenced by a moderate easing of retail inflation, particularly in food prices, though core inflation remains a watch point.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting growth while ensuring inflation remains within the target band of 2-6%.
• The central bank projected a stable GDP growth outlook for the upcoming fiscal quarter, buoyed by strong domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Liquidity measures and forward guidance were also discussed, signaling a data-dependent approach to future policy adjustments.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership Summit Concludes, Emphasizes Digital and Green Economy Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, focusing on new areas of cooperation.
• Key outcomes included agreements on enhancing collaboration in the digital economy, promoting green technologies, and fostering resilient supply chains.
• Discussions also covered regional security challenges, maritime cooperation, and counter-terrorism efforts, underscoring the importance of a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and its role as a reliable partner for peace, stability, and prosperity in the region.
• A joint statement highlighted initiatives for greater people-to-people connectivity and cultural exchanges between India and ASEAN member states.

articles.headline: Centre Launches New National Policy on Urban Waste Management to Promote Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a comprehensive National Policy on Urban Waste Management, aiming for a circular economy approach.
• The policy emphasizes source segregation, decentralized processing, and resource recovery from municipal solid waste.
• It introduces stricter enforcement mechanisms, technological integration for waste-to-energy projects, and public-private partnerships.
• A key focus is on reducing landfill dependency, promoting composting, and managing construction and demolition waste effectively.
• The policy also includes provisions for capacity building of urban local bodies and increasing public awareness regarding responsible waste disposal.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Satellite for Enhanced Climate and Weather Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth observation satellite, 'ClimateSat-1', from Sriharikota.
• ClimateSat-1 is equipped with high-resolution sensors and atmospheric sounders designed to provide crucial data for climate modeling, weather forecasting, and disaster warning systems.
• The satellite will monitor atmospheric composition, sea surface temperatures, cloud patterns, and greenhouse gas concentrations with greater precision.
• This mission reinforces India's capabilities in space-based climate observation and its commitment to global efforts in understanding climate change.
• The data from ClimateSat-1 is expected to benefit various sectors, including agriculture, maritime, and urban planning.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for Critical Infrastructure Cyber Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a comprehensive policy framework to bolster the cyber security of India's critical infrastructure sectors.
• The policy mandates regular cyber audits, implementation of advanced threat detection systems, and dedicated incident response teams for sectors like energy, finance, transport, and telecommunications.
• It emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving government agencies, private operators, and international partners, to share threat intelligence and best practices.
• A national cyber security incident response hub will be established to coordinate responses to major cyber attacks and enhance resilience.
• The framework aims to protect essential services from sophisticated cyber threats, ensuring national security and economic stability.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Unveils 'FutureReady Skills' Framework for Holistic Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education launched the 'FutureReady Skills' Framework, a comprehensive initiative aimed at integrating 21st-century skills into the national education system.
• The framework emphasizes critical thinking, creativity, collaboration, communication, and digital literacy from school to higher education levels.
• It introduces modular courses, vocational training pathways, and industry-aligned internships to bridge the skill gap and enhance employability.
• The initiative aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's vision of holistic and multidisciplinary education.
• Special provisions are made for lifelong learning opportunities and reskilling programs for the existing workforce.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Passes Multi-State Cooperative Societies (Amendment) Bill, 2025, for Enhanced Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha passed the Multi-State Cooperative Societies (Amendment) Bill, 2025, aiming to improve governance, management, and financial health of cooperative societies.
• The Bill introduces provisions for electoral reforms, transparent recruitment processes, and stricter financial discipline within multi-state cooperatives.
• It seeks to strengthen the regulatory framework and ensure greater accountability to members, preventing malpractices and enhancing efficiency.
• The amendments are expected to foster growth in the cooperative sector, which plays a crucial role in agriculture, dairy, and housing.
• The Bill now awaits consideration and passage in the Rajya Sabha.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive Reforms of UN Security Council
articles.explainer:
• India, at a high-level UN General Assembly debate, strongly reiterated its long-standing demand for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
• External Affairs Minister highlighted the need for an expanded UNSC that reflects contemporary geopolitical realities, including permanent seats for more developing countries.
• India emphasized that the current structure of the UNSC is outdated and unrepresentative, undermining its legitimacy and effectiveness in addressing global challenges.
• The call for reforms aligns with India's growing global stature and its contributions to international peace and security.
• India urged member states to move beyond procedural tactics and work towards a time-bound outcome for intergovernmental negotiations on UNSC reform.

articles.headline: Government Initiates New High-Speed Rail Corridor Project Connecting Key Economic Hubs
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Railways announced the initiation of a new high-speed rail (HSR) corridor project, linking major economic and industrial hubs in the western region.
• The project aims to significantly reduce travel time, boost regional connectivity, and facilitate faster movement of goods and passengers.
• It involves advanced railway technology, including maglev or very high-speed conventional rail systems, and substantial infrastructure investment.
• The corridor is expected to spur economic development along its route, create employment opportunities, and enhance logistical efficiency.
• Public-private partnership models are being explored for financing and execution, with environmental impact assessments underway.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Himalayan Glacier Melt, Urges Urgent Adaptation Strategies
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences revealed an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers, posing significant threats to water security.
• The study indicated a direct correlation between rising global temperatures and the shrinking volume of glaciers, impacting major river systems like the Ganga, Indus, and Brahmaputra.
• It projected increased frequency of glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) and long-term water scarcity for millions dependent on glacial meltwater.
• The report called for immediate implementation of robust adaptation strategies, including improved early warning systems, water resource management, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• International collaboration for high-altitude research and data sharing was also emphasized to mitigate the transboundary impacts.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine for Neglected Tropical Diseases
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a cluster of neglected tropical diseases (NTDs).
• The multi-valent vaccine candidate showed promising results in preclinical trials, demonstrating broad-spectrum protection against several prevalent NTDs endemic to India.
• This development is a major step towards reducing the disease burden in vulnerable populations and enhancing public health preparedness.
• The project leveraged advanced biotechnology and collaborative research between public and private institutions.
• Further clinical trials are planned to validate the vaccine's safety and efficacy, with potential for global impact.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Site in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist monastery complex during excavations in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The site includes stupas, viharas (monastic cells), intricate sculptures, and inscriptions dating back to the Gupta and post-Gupta periods.
• The findings shed new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the cultural exchanges along ancient trade routes.
• This discovery is expected to boost heritage tourism and deepen understanding of India's rich Buddhist past.
• Conservation efforts and further research are planned to preserve and study the newly found artifacts and structures.

articles.headline: Debate Intensifies on Potential Implementation of Uniform Civil Code Across States
articles.explainer:
• The debate surrounding the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) gained momentum with several state governments indicating readiness to explore its adoption.
• Discussions focused on the constitutional mandate of Article 44 and the complexities of personal laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption.
• Supporters argue UCC would promote gender justice, national integration, and simplify legal frameworks, ensuring equality before law for all citizens.
• Opponents raise concerns about its potential impact on religious freedom, cultural diversity, and the rights of minority communities.
• Legal experts and civil society organizations are engaged in extensive consultations to understand the implications and build consensus.

articles.headline: RBI Introduces Stricter Regulations for Fintech Digital Lending Platforms
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) issued new, stricter guidelines for digital lending platforms operated by Fintech companies, aiming to enhance consumer protection and curb predatory practices.
• The regulations mandate increased transparency in loan terms, disclosure of all charges, and a cooling-off period for borrowers to exit loans.
• They also address concerns regarding data privacy, mandating clear consent for data collection and prohibiting unauthorized access to personal information.
• The move comes amidst a rise in complaints against aggressive recovery tactics and exorbitant interest rates by some unregulated digital lenders.
• The new framework seeks to foster responsible innovation in the Fintech sector while safeguarding financial stability and consumer interests.

articles.headline: India Proposes G20 Roadmap for Enhanced Climate Finance Mobilization
articles.explainer:
• India, in its ongoing engagement with the G20, put forth a comprehensive roadmap for mobilizing enhanced climate finance for developing countries.
• The proposal emphasizes the need for developed nations to meet their $100 billion climate finance commitment and explores innovative funding mechanisms.
• It advocates for a just transition framework, technology transfer, and capacity building to support climate action in vulnerable economies.
• India highlighted the importance of multilateral development banks (MDBs) in scaling up green investments and de-risking private capital.
• The roadmap seeks to build consensus among G20 members ahead of upcoming global climate summits, pushing for equitable and accessible climate funding.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Recommends Enhanced National Framework for Gender Budgeting
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a policy brief recommending a strengthened national framework for gender budgeting across all government ministries and departments.
• The recommendations aim to ensure that public expenditure and policies are formulated with a clear understanding of their differential impact on men and women.
• It proposes mandatory gender impact assessments for major schemes, disaggregated data collection, and increased allocation for women-centric programs.
• The move is intended to promote gender equality, empower women, and bridge existing gender gaps in various socio-economic indicators.
• NITI Aayog emphasized the need for capacity building among policymakers and a robust monitoring mechanism for effective implementation.

articles.headline: India Reviews Implementation of Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Climate Change Concerns
articles.explainer:
• India initiated a comprehensive review of the implementation of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing concerns over the impact of climate change on water flows.
• The review focuses on optimizing India's usage of its share of waters from the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) for irrigation and hydropower projects.
• Discussions also touch upon the need for enhanced data sharing and cooperation with Pakistan to address new challenges posed by glacial melt and extreme weather events.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, has largely withstood geopolitical tensions, but evolving environmental factors necessitate a re-evaluation.
• India reiterated its commitment to the spirit of the treaty while asserting its rights for full utilization of allocated waters for development purposes.

• The scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing capabilities, reduce import dependence, and create a robust ecosystem for solar energy production.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. Its primary objective is to promote the use of grey hydrogen in industrial applications.
2. Phase II of the mission focuses on domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and establishing green hydrogen corridors.
3. India aims to become a net exporter of green hydrogen under this mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The mission's primary objective is to promote green hydrogen, not grey hydrogen (which is produced from fossil fuels without carbon capture). Statement 2 is correct, as Phase II emphasizes scaling up production, domestic manufacturing of electrolysers, and establishing green hydrogen corridors. Statement 3 is also correct, as the mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.

prelimsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court ruling on electoral bonds primarily addresses which of the following issues?
options: (a) Limiting election expenditure by political parties.
(b) Ensuring transparency in the funding of political parties.
(c) Regulating the use of social media during elections.
(d) Mandating internal democracy within political parties.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Ensuring transparency in the funding of political parties.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's ruling specifically mandated the Election Commission of India to ensure complete disclosure of all electoral bond transactions, including donor identities and recipient political parties, to enhance transparency in electoral funding. While other options are related to electoral reforms, the ruling's direct focus was on transparency in funding.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rates in India, such as the Repo Rate?
options: (a) Ministry of Finance
(b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) through its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
(d) Indian Banks' Association (IBA)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) through its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is entrusted with the task of setting the policy repo rate to achieve the inflation target, while keeping in mind the objective of growth. The Ministry of Finance handles fiscal policy, SEBI regulates securities markets, and IBA is an association of banks.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT):
1. The Treaty was brokered by the United Nations in 1960.
2. Under the Treaty, India has exclusive use of the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej).
3. Pakistan has exclusive use of the waters of the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 1 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The Indus Waters Treaty was brokered by the World Bank (then the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development), not the United Nations. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, outlining the allocation of rivers to India and Pakistan respectively for exclusive use, with certain provisions for limited use by the other country.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Circular Economy', often associated with new waste management policies, primarily implies:
options: (a) Promoting linear production models for efficiency.
(b) Maximizing the extraction of virgin raw materials.
(c) Designing out waste and pollution, keeping products and materials in use, and regenerating natural systems.
(d) Focusing solely on incineration of waste to generate energy.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Designing out waste and pollution, keeping products and materials in use, and regenerating natural systems.
prelimsMocks.explanation: A circular economy is an economic system aimed at eliminating waste and the continual use of resources. It involves designing products for durability, reuse, and recyclability, thereby keeping materials in use for as long as possible and regenerating natural systems. Options (a) and (b) describe linear economy principles, and (d) is one aspect of waste management but not the core definition of a circular economy.

GS1.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Himalayan Glacier Melt, Urges Urgent Adaptation Strategies
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences revealed an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers, posing significant threats to water security.
• The study indicated a direct correlation between rising global temperatures and the shrinking volume of glaciers, impacting major river systems like the Ganga, Indus, and Brahmaputra.
• It projected increased frequency of glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) and long-term water scarcity for millions dependent on glacial meltwater.
• The report called for immediate implementation of robust adaptation strategies, including improved early warning systems, water resource management, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• International collaboration for high-altitude research and data sharing was also emphasized to mitigate the transboundary impacts.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Site in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist monastery complex during excavations in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The site includes stupas, viharas (monastic cells), intricate sculptures, and inscriptions dating back to the Gupta and post-Gupta periods.
• The findings shed new light on the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the cultural exchanges along ancient trade routes.
• This discovery is expected to boost heritage tourism and deepen understanding of India's rich Buddhist past.
• Conservation efforts and further research are planned to preserve and study the newly found artifacts and structures.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Full Disclosure of Electoral Bond Data to Enhance Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, mandated the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure complete disclosure of all electoral bond transactions, including donor identities and recipient political parties.
• The judgment emphasized the citizens' right to information and the importance of transparency in electoral funding to uphold free and fair elections.
• This decision comes after a series of petitions challenging the anonymity provisions of the Electoral Bond Scheme, citing concerns over potential quid pro quo arrangements.
• The Court directed the ECI to publish all past and future data on its official website within a stipulated timeframe.
• The ruling is expected to significantly impact political funding practices and accountability mechanisms in India.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Unveils 'FutureReady Skills' Framework for Holistic Development
GS2.explainer:

• The framework emphasizes critical thinking, creativity, collaboration, communication, and digital literacy from school to higher education levels.

GS2.headline: Lok Sabha Passes Multi-State Cooperative Societies (Amendment) Bill, 2025, for Enhanced Governance
GS2.explainer:

• The Bill introduces provisions for electoral reforms, transparent recruitment processes, and stricter financial discipline within multi-state cooperatives.
• It seeks to strengthen the regulatory framework and ensure greater accountability to members, preventing malpractices and enhancing efficiency.
• The amendments are expected to foster growth in the cooperative sector, which plays a crucial role in agriculture, dairy, and housing.
• The Bill now awaits consideration and passage in the Rajya Sabha.

• India urged member states to move beyond procedural tactics and work towards a time-bound outcome for intergovernmental negotiations on UNSC reform.

• Opponents raise concerns about its potential impact on religious freedom, cultural diversity, and the rights of minority communities.
• Legal experts and civil society organizations are engaged in extensive consultations to understand the implications and build consensus.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Global Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and enhancing domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key components include incentives for green hydrogen production, establishment of green hydrogen corridors, and support for R&D in electrolysis technology.
• This phase emphasizes skill development and job creation in the emerging green hydrogen ecosystem across the country.
• The move aligns with India's ambitious climate targets and its vision for 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in the energy sector.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level, citing a cautious approach to economic recovery.
• The decision was influenced by a moderate easing of retail inflation, particularly in food prices, though core inflation remains a watch point.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting growth while ensuring inflation remains within the target band of 2-6%.
• The central bank projected a stable GDP growth outlook for the upcoming fiscal quarter, buoyed by strong domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Liquidity measures and forward guidance were also discussed, signaling a data-dependent approach to future policy adjustments.

GS3.headline: Centre Launches New National Policy on Urban Waste Management to Promote Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a comprehensive National Policy on Urban Waste Management, aiming for a circular economy approach.
• The policy emphasizes source segregation, decentralized processing, and resource recovery from municipal solid waste.
• It introduces stricter enforcement mechanisms, technological integration for waste-to-energy projects, and public-private partnerships.
• A key focus is on reducing landfill dependency, promoting composting, and managing construction and demolition waste effectively.
• The policy also includes provisions for capacity building of urban local bodies and increasing public awareness regarding responsible waste disposal.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Satellite for Enhanced Climate and Weather Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth observation satellite, 'ClimateSat-1', from Sriharikota.
• ClimateSat-1 is equipped with high-resolution sensors and atmospheric sounders designed to provide crucial data for climate modeling, weather forecasting, and disaster warning systems.
• The satellite will monitor atmospheric composition, sea surface temperatures, cloud patterns, and greenhouse gas concentrations with greater precision.
• This mission reinforces India's capabilities in space-based climate observation and its commitment to global efforts in understanding climate change.
• The data from ClimateSat-1 is expected to benefit various sectors, including agriculture, maritime, and urban planning.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for Critical Infrastructure Cyber Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a comprehensive policy framework to bolster the cyber security of India's critical infrastructure sectors.
• The policy mandates regular cyber audits, implementation of advanced threat detection systems, and dedicated incident response teams for sectors like energy, finance, transport, and telecommunications.
• It emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving government agencies, private operators, and international partners, to share threat intelligence and best practices.
• A national cyber security incident response hub will be established to coordinate responses to major cyber attacks and enhance resilience.
• The framework aims to protect essential services from sophisticated cyber threats, ensuring national security and economic stability.

GS3.headline: Government Initiates New High-Speed Rail Corridor Project Connecting Key Economic Hubs
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Railways announced the initiation of a new high-speed rail (HSR) corridor project, linking major economic and industrial hubs in the western region.
• The project aims to significantly reduce travel time, boost regional connectivity, and facilitate faster movement of goods and passengers.
• It involves advanced railway technology, including maglev or very high-speed conventional rail systems, and substantial infrastructure investment.
• The corridor is expected to spur economic development along its route, create employment opportunities, and enhance logistical efficiency.
• Public-private partnership models are being explored for financing and execution, with environmental impact assessments underway.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine for Neglected Tropical Diseases
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a cluster of neglected tropical diseases (NTDs).
• The multi-valent vaccine candidate showed promising results in preclinical trials, demonstrating broad-spectrum protection against several prevalent NTDs endemic to India.
• This development is a major step towards reducing the disease burden in vulnerable populations and enhancing public health preparedness.
• The project leveraged advanced biotechnology and collaborative research between public and private institutions.
• Further clinical trials are planned to validate the vaccine's safety and efficacy, with potential for global impact.

GS3.headline: RBI Introduces Stricter Regulations for Fintech Digital Lending Platforms
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) issued new, stricter guidelines for digital lending platforms operated by Fintech companies, aiming to enhance consumer protection and curb predatory practices.
• The regulations mandate increased transparency in loan terms, disclosure of all charges, and a cooling-off period for borrowers to exit loans.
• They also address concerns regarding data privacy, mandating clear consent for data collection and prohibiting unauthorized access to personal information.
• The move comes amidst a rise in complaints against aggressive recovery tactics and exorbitant interest rates by some unregulated digital lenders.
• The new framework seeks to foster responsible innovation in the Fintech sector while safeguarding financial stability and consumer interests.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Solar PV Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for 'High-Efficiency Solar PV Modules' manufacturing in India.
• The scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing capabilities, reduce import dependence, and create a robust ecosystem for solar energy production.
• Incentives will be provided for setting up integrated manufacturing units across the entire value chain, from polysilicon to solar modules.
• This initiative is critical for achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and enhancing energy security.
• It is expected to attract significant investments, generate employment, and position India as a global leader in solar technology.
